title: Ch 10.3 — Advanced Streams: Numeric Operations and Chaining
description:
Practice mapToInt, sum, max, average, sorted, limit, and collect.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Background
──────────────────────────────────────────────

Numeric streams let you calculate totals, extremes, and averages:

  int total = list.stream()
                  .mapToInt(x -> x.getValue())
                  .sum();

  OptionalInt biggest = list.stream()
                            .mapToInt(x -> x.getValue())
                            .max();
  if (biggest.isPresent()) System.out.println(biggest.getAsInt());

To collect sorted results into a list:

  List<String> names = list.stream()
      .sorted(Comparator.comparing(x -> x.getValue()))
      .map(x -> x.getName())
      .collect(Collectors.toList());

For descending order, chain .reversed() on the Comparator:

  Comparator.comparing(x -> x.getValue()).reversed()

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Class: AdvancedStreamExercise
──────────────────────────────────────────────

Replace each  // TODO  line with a stream expression.
Do NOT change variable names or print statements.

The Mermaid class has: getName(), getAge(), getOcean()
The Ship class has:    getName(), getSpeed(), getCargoCapacity()

(1)  int totalAge
       Total age of ALL mermaids. Use mapToInt + sum.

(2)  int oldestAge
       The highest age among ALL mermaids.
       Use mapToInt + max. Call .getAsInt() on the result.

(3)  double avgSpeed
       Average speed of ALL ships.
       Use mapToInt + average. Call .getAsDouble() on the result.

(4)  int fastCapacity
       Total cargo capacity of ships whose speed > 20.
       Use filter + mapToInt + sum.

(5)  List<String> oldestThree
       Names of the 3 oldest mermaids, sorted from oldest to youngest.
       Use sorted with Comparator.comparing(...).reversed(),
       then limit(3), then map to name, then collect.

(6)  int minFastSpeed
       Minimum speed among ships whose speed > 20.
       Use filter + mapToInt + min. Call .getAsInt() on the result.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
HINTS
──────────────────────────────────────────────
• .mapToInt() returns an IntStream with .sum(), .max(), .min(),
  and .average() built in.
• .max() and .min() return OptionalInt; call .getAsInt().
• .average() returns OptionalDouble; call .getAsDouble().
• For (5), the import for Comparator and Collectors is already there.
